steps.title: Use the given root as a plug-in shortcut
steps.content:
The question gives a solution, so you do not need to derive the whole quadratic formula. Let
$$r=\frac{-27+\sqrt{261}}{26}$$
Because $r$ is a solution of $x(13x+27)+c=5$, the correct answer choice for $c$ makes $r(13r+27)+c$ equal $5$.

steps.title: Check the choices in Desmos
steps.content: Enter $r$, then test the four possible values of $c$. Only $c=14$ makes the left side equal $5$; the other choices produce different values.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content:
If you expand first, $x(13x+27)+c=5$ becomes $13x^2+27x+(c-5)=0$. Matching the given quadratic-form root means the discriminant is $261$:
$$27^2-4(13)(c-5)=261$$
$$989-52c=261$$
$$c=14$$
That matches choice A.
